text: When a rescue vessel approaches a lifeboat in heavy seas, the person in charge of the lifeboat should:

options.text: Immediately order everyone to jump into the sea
Wait for calmer weather or specific instructions before transferring personnel
Tie the lifeboat directly to the rescue ship's propeller
Turn off the lifeboat engine and abandon helm controls

explanation: Transferring personnel from a lifeboat to a rescue vessel in heavy seas is extremely dangerous; the person in charge must prioritize safety and await favorable conditions or specific instructions to prevent injury or loss of life.
